pattern: Binary search on the answer combined with a greedy heap-based feasibility check: use a min-heap to always zero-out the index with the largest nums value at each changeIndices opportunity.
hints: Binary search on the answer T in [1, m]; to check feasibility within T seconds, scan changeIndices[0..T-1] from right to left.

When we encounter the last occurrence of index i in changeIndices[0..T-1], we may choose to spend that second zero-ing it (free) instead of decrementing; greedily 'buy' the most expensive remaining index (use a max-heap of nums values for unmark indices that appear in changeIndices).
Each 'buy' (zero-out) costs 1 second and 1 marking second; if we don't buy, we get 1 free decrement second; track whether total free decrements + bought indices covers all requirements.

edge_inputs.description: all nums zero — only marking seconds needed
impossible — not enough total seconds for all decrements and markings
single large-value element; zero-out via changeIndices is far cheaper than decrementing
three elements needing zero-out, barely enough seconds
decrement vs zero-out tradeoff: some indices have nums=0 so zero-out does nothing useful
changeIndices never covers some indices — those must be decremented the slow way

stress.description: random small nums and changeIndices arrays
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(size, 15))
    m = size
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 10) for _ in range(n)]
    changeIndices = [rng.randint(1, n) for _ in range(m)]
    # Ensure all indices appear
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        changeIndices[rng.randint(0, m - 1)] = i
    return [nums, changeIndices]
